story.iWant: PostgreSQL RLS policies enforcing tenant isolation at database level
story.soThat: even SQL injection or bugs cannot breach tenant boundaries (Layer 3)
story.tasks: - AC1: Flyway migration V004__rls_policies.sql enables RLS on all tenant-scoped tables - AC2: RLS policies created: widget_view table requires tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id') - AC3: PostgreSQL session variable set by JooqConfiguration before queries - AC4: RLS policies tested: attempt cross-tenant query → returns empty result - AC5: Integration test validates Layer 3 blocks unauthorized access - AC6: Performance impact measured (<2ms overhead per query) - AC7: RLS policies documented in docs/reference/multi-tenancy.md

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Flyway migration V004__rls_policies.sql enables RLS on all tenant-scoped tables 2. RLS policies created: widget_view table requires tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id') 3. PostgreSQL session variable set by JooqConfiguration before queries 4. RLS policies tested: attempt cross-tenant query → returns empty result 5. Integration test validates Layer 3 blocks unauthorized access 6. Performance impact measured (<2ms overhead per query) 7. RLS policies documented in docs/reference/multi-tenancy.md

doc.relevance: Defines Layer 3 of 3-layer tenant isolation: database-level enforcement with Row-Level Security
doc.snippet: Layer 3: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (Database Enforcement) - Enable RLS on all tenant-scoped tables (ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY) - Create RLS policies: CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON table FOR ALL USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')) - Session variable app.tenant_id set by jOOQ configuration before each query - Even SQL injection cannot bypass RLS (PostgreSQL kernel enforcement) - Performance target: <2ms overhead per query (BRIN indexes for tenant_id) - RLS provides defense against application bugs and injection attacks

doc.section: Section 3 (FR004 - RLS), Section 4.2 (Projection Schema with RLS)
doc.snippet: PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce tenant isolation at database level. Session variable app.tenant_id propagated from TenantContext via jOOQ. Projection tables (widget_view) include tenant_id column with RLS policies. Performance: BRIN indexes on tenant_id for time-series query optimization.

doc.relevance: BRIN indexes and partitioning for event store performance
doc.snippet: Mandatory PostgreSQL optimizations: - BRIN Indexes: Time-based indexing for event streams and tenant_id - Time-based Partitioning: Monthly partitions on domain_event_entry table - Connection Pooling: HikariCP with optimal settings - Performance Targets: API p95 <200ms, query overhead <2ms

doc.relevance: Business requirements for database-level tenant isolation
doc.snippet: Layer 3: Database-level enforcement prevents data leaks even with application bugs. PostgreSQL RLS provides kernel-level isolation guarantees. Defense against SQL injection and application vulnerabilities.

Story 4.4: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security Policies - Layer 3 - Flyway migration V004__rls_policies.sql enables RLS on all tenant-scoped tables - RLS policies: widget_view table requires tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id') - PostgreSQL session variable set by JooqConfiguration before queries - Performance impact measured (<2ms overhead per query)

artifact.reason: Story 4.3 validates at service layer; Story 4.4 adds database layer
Existing Flyway migrations for V001, V002, V003 from Epic 1/2
jOOQ configuration for setting PostgreSQL session variables
widget_view projection table to be enhanced with tenant_id column + RLS

dependency.name: jOOQ
dependency.purpose: SQL query builder with session variable support

constraints.constraint: RLS policy pattern for all tables: CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON table_name FOR ALL USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')); Policy applies to SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE operations.

Set app.tenant_id session variable BEFORE every query. Use jOOQ ExecuteListener or DSLContext configuration. Session variable: SET LOCAL app.tenant_id = '<tenant-id>'; Extract tenant_id from TenantContext.getCurrentTenantId().
RLS overhead must be <2ms per query. Use BRIN indexes on tenant_id column for optimization. Measure with performance tests (baseline vs RLS-enabled).

Missing app.tenant_id session variable → RLS returns empty result (fail-safe). PostgreSQL current_setting('app.tenant_id') throws error if not set. Use current_setting('app.tenant_id', true) for null-safe default.

interface.name: Flyway RLS Migration
interface.description: SQL migration to enable RLS and create tenant isolation policies. Migration file: V004__rls_policies.sql

interface.name: jOOQ Session Variable Configuration
interface.description: jOOQ ExecuteListener to set PostgreSQL session variable before queries. Extracts tenant_id from TenantContext and propagates to database.

interface.name: RLS Integration Testing
interface.description: Integration test pattern to validate RLS blocks cross-tenant access. Use direct JDBC queries to bypass application layer.

standards.standard: Constitutional TDD: Integration test BEFORE RLS implementation. Test validates database-level tenant isolation with direct SQL.

Test all RLS scenarios: - Tenant A queries with app.tenant_id='tenant-a' → sees only Tenant A data - Tenant A queries with app.tenant_id='tenant-b' → sees only Tenant B data - Query without app.tenant_id set → returns empty result (fail-safe) - Direct SQL bypassing application → RLS still enforces isolation
Performance test: measure query latency with/without RLS. Baseline: query without RLS RLS-enabled: same query with RLS policy Validate: overhead <2ms (acceptance criteria)
Use Testcontainers PostgreSQL for integration tests. Real PostgreSQL with RLS support (not H2 - H2 explicitly forbidden). Apply Flyway migrations including V004__rls_policies.sql.

ideas.idea: Test AC1: Verify RLS enabled on widget_view (pg_catalog.pg_tables query)
Test AC2: Verify RLS policy exists (pg_policies system catalog)
Test AC3: Verify session variable set before queries (jOOQ ExecuteListener)
Test AC4: Direct SQL cross-tenant query → empty result (RLS enforcement)
Test AC5: Integration test - create widgets for tenant-a, query as tenant-b → no results
Test AC6: Performance test - measure query latency baseline vs RLS-enabled
Test missing session variable → empty result (fail-safe with current_setting null-safe)
Test BRIN index performance on tenant_id column
Test RLS with concurrent multi-tenant queries (thread safety)
Property-based test: random tenant IDs, verify isolation invariant
